headline: Mexico and South Korea Kick Off the Carnival with a Dose of Reality
theDrama: Mexico looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park against South Africa, cruising to a 2-0 win that was about as stressful as a Sunday morning stroll. Meanwhile, South Korea and Czechia decided to actually play some football, with the Koreans edging it 2-1 in a game that had more bite than a Sunday League tackle in the mud.
mustWatchHighlights: Watch the South Korea vs Czechia match if you enjoy a bit of end-to-end chaos. As for the Mexico game, unless you enjoy watching one side dominate possession while the other looks like they've forgotten how to string two passes together, you might as well put your feet up and have a nap.
progressionNews: It is day one, so keep your shirts on. Every single team in Group A is still in the hunt, and the rest of the tournament hasn't even kicked a ball yet, so everyone remains in contention.

headline: USA Turns Up the Heat While Canada and Bosnia Trade Paint in a Tactical Snoozefest
theDrama: The Yanks put on a proper show at SoFi, dismantling Paraguay with the kind of clinical finishing that makes you wonder if they've been practicing in secret. Meanwhile, up in Toronto, Canada and Bosnia-Herzegovina played out a 1-1 draw that had all the excitement of watching paint dry in a damp basement. It was the sort of game where you'd rather be doing the ironing than watching the final ten minutes.
mustWatchHighlights: Definitely catch the USA highlights if you enjoy seeing a team actually bother to shoot on target. As for the Canada-Bosnia affair, do yourself a favour and put your feet up with a cuppa instead—it was a masterclass in how to waste ninety minutes of everyone's life.
progressionNews: It is still very early doors, and every single team across all groups remains in contention. Nobody is booking their flights home just yet, as every side still has plenty of football left to play to secure their spot in the knockouts.

headline: Brazil Stumble and Scotland Scrape It: A Day of World Cup Mediocrity
theDrama: Brazil looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park against Morocco, failing to break down a side that clearly didn't read the script. Meanwhile, Switzerland managed a staggering 26 shots against Qatar and still only walked away with a point—that is Sunday League finishing at its finest. Scotland, bless them, did the bare minimum to edge past Haiti, proving that sometimes you don't need to play well, you just need to be slightly less rubbish than the other lot.
mustWatchHighlights: If you enjoy watching professional footballers miss the target from six yards out, the Switzerland-Qatar match is your highlight reel. As for the rest, Brazil’s struggle was a proper snooze-fest for anyone expecting a samba masterclass. Put your feet up and skip the replays unless you’re a glutton for punishment.
progressionNews: It is early days in this tournament, and every single team across the groups remains in contention. Nobody is booking flights home just yet, and nobody has secured their spot in the next round. Everyone is still in the hunt, so keep your shirts on—there is plenty of football left to be played before anyone starts popping the champagne or packing their bags.

headline: Germany Turns Curaçao Into A Training Cone Drill While The Dutch And Japanese Play A Polite Game Of Tag
theDrama: Germany decided to treat their opener like a Sunday morning kickabout, putting seven past Curaçao in a display of absolute clinical brutality. Meanwhile, the Netherlands and Japan decided that defending was entirely optional, serving up a 2-2 draw that had all the tactical discipline of a pub team after three pints. Australia looked sharp against a toothless Türkiye, and Sweden absolutely dismantled Tunisia in a performance that makes you wonder if the Tunisians forgot their boots at the hotel.
mustWatchHighlights: Watch the German masterclass if you enjoy watching a professional side bully the playground kids. Avoid the Netherlands versus Japan match unless you have a burning desire to see what happens when two teams decide that marking is just a suggestion.
progressionNews: It is still very early days, and every single team across all groups remains in the hunt and can still qualify for the knockout stages. Nobody is booking their flights home just yet, as everyone is currently in contention with plenty of football left to be played.

headline: Spain and Belgium Stumble: The World Cup's Opening Weekend of Absolute Snoozefests
theDrama: It was a day for the purists, if your idea of purity is watching world-class squads struggle to find the back of the net against the underdogs. Spain managed a staggering 27 shots against Cape Verde and still couldn't buy a goal, while Belgium looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park against Egypt. Meanwhile, the Iran-New Zealand clash was the only bit of actual entertainment, ending in a 2-2 draw that felt like a proper cup tie.
mustWatchHighlights: If you value your sleep, avoid the Spain-Cape Verde match at all costs; it was a masterclass in how to waste 90 minutes. The Iran versus New Zealand game was the only one worth a look, providing a rare bit of excitement in an otherwise dreary day of football.
progressionNews: It is early days in this tournament, and every single team across Groups A through L remains in contention. Nobody has been sent packing just yet, so everyone is still in the hunt and has plenty of games left to prove they aren't just here for the holiday.

headline: France and Argentina Flex Their Muscles While Iraq Gets a Reality Check
theDrama: France looked like they were having a casual kickabout against Senegal, putting three past them with clinical ease. Meanwhile, Argentina reminded everyone why they are the ones to beat, dismantling Algeria with a performance that was pure liquid football. As for Iraq, well, they were on the wrong end of a 4-1 hammering by Norway—a proper Sunday League defensive display that left them chasing shadows all afternoon.
mustWatchHighlights: The France-Senegal clash was the one to catch if you enjoy seeing a side turn on the style. As for the Iraq-Norway game, unless you have a penchant for watching a team get picked apart like a Sunday roast, I’d suggest you put your feet up and skip the highlights.
progressionNews: It is still very early days, and every single team in Groups I and J remains in the hunt. Nobody is booking flights home just yet, as every side is still in contention to finish in the top two and secure their spot in the knockouts.

headline: England Put on a Show While Portugal Stumble in the Texas Heat
theDrama: England decided to actually turn up for once, putting four past Croatia in a display that almost looked like professional football. Meanwhile, Portugal had 75% possession against Congo DR and still couldn't find a way to win, proving once again that having the ball is useless if you've got the finishing ability of a Sunday League pub team. Austria looked tidy against Jordan, and Colombia did the business against Uzbekistan, but the real drama was watching Portugal try to pass the ball into the net for 90 minutes.
mustWatchHighlights: The England-Croatia game was the only one worth the price of admission, unless you enjoy watching Portugal struggle to break down a stubborn defense. If you want to see what a proper snooze-fest looks like, go back and watch the Portugal-Congo DR highlights; it was like watching paint dry in slow motion.
progressionNews: It is still very early days, and every single team across Groups J, K, and L remains in contention. Nobody has booked their flights home just yet, and nobody has secured their spot in the next round. Everyone is still in the hunt, so keep your shirts on—there is plenty of football left to be played.

headline: Canada Turns Qatar Into A Training Cone Drill While Mexico Cruises To The Knockouts
theDrama: Canada absolutely dismantled Qatar in a 6-0 demolition that looked more like a Sunday League side playing against professionals. It was total liquid football from the Canadians, while Qatar looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park. Elsewhere, Mexico secured their spot in the next round with a gritty win over South Korea, and Switzerland put four past Bosnia-Herzegovina to confirm their progression in a display of clinical finishing.
mustWatchHighlights: The Canada versus Qatar match is the one to watch if you enjoy watching a team get absolutely dismantled. Avoid the Czechia versus South Africa stalemate at all costs; it was the footballing equivalent of watching paint dry in the rain.
progressionNews: Mexico and Canada have officially qualified for the knockout stages, showing the rest of the field how it is done. Switzerland has also secured their place in the next round. Meanwhile, Bosnia-Herzegovina and Qatar are in the 'needs points' category, meaning they need a strong result in their final group games to keep their best-3rd place hopes alive. Every other team in the tournament remains in contention, with plenty of twists and turns still to come.

headline: USA Cruises While Turkey Implodes: A Day of Absolute Scenes and Sunday League Finishing
theDrama: The USA looked like a walking cheat code against Australia, while Turkey managed to fire 32 shots at the Paraguayan goal without finding the net once—a masterclass in how to make a mockery of the beautiful game. Meanwhile, Scotland’s dreams took a proper battering against Morocco, and Brazil finally decided to show up, putting three past a hapless Haiti side.
mustWatchHighlights: Watch the USA vs Australia match if you want to see a team actually know where the goal is. As for Turkey vs Paraguay, do yourself a favour and put your feet up instead; watching 32 shots miss the target is enough to make any self-respecting pundit weep into their lukewarm tea.
progressionNews: The USA, Mexico, Canada, and Switzerland have officially qualified for the knockouts, so they can start planning their victory parades. Haiti and Turkey have been sent packing and are officially eliminated, meaning they can start booking their flights home. Everyone else in the groups mentioned remains in the hunt or is fighting for those precious best-3rd place slots, with Bosnia-Herzegovina and Qatar needing points to keep their tournament pulse beating.

headline: Dutch Delight, German Efficiency, and an Absolute Snoozefest in Kansas City
theDrama: The Dutch turned up and absolutely dismantled Sweden, looking like a proper side for once, while Germany squeezed past Ivory Coast in a game that had more tension than a penalty shootout in the rain. Meanwhile, Ecuador and Curaçao decided that scoring goals was strictly forbidden, serving up a 0-0 bore draw that had me checking if my telly was actually switched on.
mustWatchHighlights: Watch the Netherlands masterclass if you want to see how it's done, but for the love of all that is holy, avoid the Ecuador-Curaçao highlights unless you're struggling with insomnia and need a cure.
progressionNews: Germany has officially punched their ticket to the knockouts with a clinical display. Haiti and Türkiye are officially booking their flights home after being eliminated. Bosnia-Herzegovina and Qatar are in the waiting room, needing points to keep their best-3rd place dreams alive, while everyone else in these groups remains in the hunt for a spot in the next round.

headline: Spain Turn on the Style While Belgium Put Us All to Sleep
theDrama: Spain finally decided to show up and dismantled Saudi Arabia with the clinical precision of a surgeon, while Belgium and Iran treated us to a masterclass in how to bore a stadium full of people to tears. Tunisia are officially heading for the airport after Japan gave them a proper schooling, and Uruguay’s draw with Cape Verde was about as chaotic as a Sunday League side after three pints at halftime.
mustWatchHighlights: Watch the Spain masterclass if you enjoy actual football, but for the love of all that is holy, avoid the Belgium-Iran stalemate unless you suffer from chronic insomnia and need a cure.
progressionNews: Mexico, Canada, Switzerland, USA, Germany, and the Netherlands have officially qualified for the knockouts. Haiti, Türkiye, and Tunisia are officially eliminated and can start booking their flights home. Bosnia-Herzegovina and Qatar need points to keep their best-3rd hopes alive, while everyone else remains in the hunt and in contention to progress.

headline: Messi and Mbappé Run Riot as the Big Boys Flex Their Muscles
theDrama: A day of clinical finishes and defensive disasters. Argentina and France look like they're just having a casual kickabout in the park, while Norway's Haaland is making a mockery of international defenses.
mustWatchHighlights: Norway vs Senegal was the pick of the bunch for pure, unadulterated chaos.
progressionNews: Argentina, France, and Norway have officially qualified. Iraq, Senegal, and Jordan are eliminated and can start booking their flights home.
